water.name: American River, North Fork, Middle Fork, South Fork and their tributaries above Folsom Lake

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(4) — American River, North Fork, Middle Fork, South Fork and tributaries above Folsom Lake
sources.quotedText: (4) American River, North Fork, Middle Fork, South Fork and their tributaries above Folsom Lake (Placer, El Dorado, Amador, and Alpine Cos.), except Caples Creek) (See subsection (b)(24)). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout Oct. 1 through the Fri. preceding Memorial Day. Only artificial lures with barbless hook may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: Region: R2; County: El Dorado; Release Water: American River Silver Fork; Species: Rainbow Trout; Release Size: Catchable; Release Program: Put and Take; Release Month Range: June-September. Region: R2; County: El Dorado; Release Water: American River, South Fork, Camp Sacramento; Species: Rainbow Trout; Release Size: Catchable; Release Program: Put and Take; Release Month Range: May-August. Region: R2; County: El Dorado; Release Water: American River, South Fork, Chili Bar; Species: Rainbow Trout; Release Size: Catchable; Release Program: Put and Take; Release Month Range: April-May & September-October.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 267066, "North Fork American River", Stream, CA (El Dorado/Placer/Sacramento Cos.)
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":267066,"gaz_name":"North Fork American River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA"} — geometry first point (Web Mercator -13485857.056, 4680440.889) converts to lon -121.1455, lat 38.7116, the North Fork's mouth where it enters Folsom Lake near Auburn (matches Wikipedia's infobox coordinate 38.71167, -121.14556 for the same confluence/mouth). The prior recorded coordinate (-121.067, 38.907) was about 23 km east-northeast, near Foresthill/the North Fork canyon interior rather than the lake-adjacent boundary implied by "above Folsom Lake," and has been corrected to this GNIS-backed mouth point, used here as the representative point for the compound multi-fork entry.

parameters.description: This entry excludes Caples Creek, which is separately regulated under CCR §7.50(b)(24).

regulations.humanSummary: Caples Creek is excluded from this entry and is regulated separately
Open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30 (5-trout limit); open October 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day for artificial-lure, barbless-hook catch-and-release only
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout Oct. 1 through the Fri. preceding Memorial Day. Only artificial lures with barbless hook may be used. 0 trout

parameters.counting_rule: Daily limit of 5 trout, possession limit of 10 (twice the daily limit per statewide default §5.85), during the Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30 season.

regulations.humanSummary: 5-trout daily bag limit, Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, October 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day.

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks only, October 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hook may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Zero trout may be retained October 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day; all trout caught must be released unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout bag limit (catch and release only), October 1 through the Friday preceding Memorial Day

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document lists catchable Rainbow Trout stocked into 'American River Silver Fork' (El Dorado Co., a South Fork American River tributary) under the Put and Take program, release month range June-September. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document lists catchable Rainbow Trout stocked into 'American River, South Fork, Camp Sacramento' (El Dorado Co.) under the Put and Take program, release month range May-August. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document lists catchable Rainbow Trout stocked into 'American River, South Fork, Chili Bar' (El Dorado Co.) under the Put and Take program, release month range April-May & September-October (spring window shown here). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document lists catchable Rainbow Trout stocked into 'American River, South Fork, Chili Bar' (El Dorado Co.) under the Put and Take program, release month range April-May & September-October (fall window shown here). This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
